params.name: Antikythera Mechanism
params.description:
On May 10, 1900 AD, a diver discovered the mechanism on a sunken ancient Greek ship near the island of Antikythera.
The Antikythera mechanism comes from an ancient Greek shipwreck. It is a bronze mechanical computer that consists of a portable planetary model made of gears and wheels, which can predict the positions of stars and planets, as well as demonstrate solar and lunar eclipses. When it was first discovered, it had been severely corroded by seawater, and scientists were only able to un …
